body: I passed some of the longer code snippets through Prettier using the [Prettier Playground](https://prettier.io/playground/#-SDNgBJKARWAkMCswqhGskGA6fF+uAAXy7QA), in an attempt to clean up some of the horizontal scroll in the rendered documentation pages.
author: curran

body:
```suggestion
d3.groupSort(
  penguins,
  (D) => d3.median(D, (d) => d.body_mass_g),
  (d) => d.species
); // ["Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo"]
```
author: curran

body:
```suggestion
d3.groupSort(
  penguins,
  (D) => -d3.median(D, (d) => d.body_mass_g),
  (d) => d.species
); // ["Gentoo", "Adelie", "Chinstrap"]
```
author: curran

author: Fil

body: This will append a new axis every time it renders, right? Because every time DotPlot renders, it’ll create a new `x` scale, triggering this effect. I think either you have to remove the axis you added in a disposal hook for the useEffect, or you have to use a join to replace the previous axis (if any) when the effect runs. 🤔

Thanks.

I still really want a script we can run against the Markdown at build time so that we can prevent broken links in the future! I think we could do it with just regular expressions if we had to. (If not whatever Markdown parser VitePress uses.)
author: mbostock

author: mbostock

body: Great idea! I suggested a change to the commit to also mention `d3-drag` and `d3-zoom`, which are also modules that required direct DOM access.
author: curran

body:
```suggestion
D3 modules that operate on [selections](./d3-selection/selecting.md) (including [d3-selection](./d3-selection.md), [d3-transition](./d3-transition.md), [d3-axis](./d3-axis.md), [d3-drag](./d3-drag.md), [d3-zoom](./d3-zoom.md)) do manipulate the DOM, which competes with React’s virtual DOM. In those cases, you can attach a ref to an element and pass it to D3 in a `useEffect` hook. (See [sandbox](https://codesandbox.io/s/d3-react-useeffect-5lp0x6?file=/src/LinePlot.jsx).)
```
author: curran

body: This list isn’t intended to be exhaustive, and I don’t want to set the expectation that we update it if we (theoretically) were to add another module in the future which touched the DOM. It’s just an illustrative list.
author: mbostock

body: I’m not comfortable paraphrasing what other people said and presenting it as a quote. We could say that we’re paraphrasing, but that would nullify the benefit of simplifying the quote. So I say we leave it as is.

author: mbostock

body:
```suggestion
This module implements a [velocity Verlet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verlet_integration) numerical integrator for simulating physical forces on particles. Force simulations can be used to visualize [networks](https://observablehq.com/@d3/force-directed-graph/2?intent=fork) and [hierarchies](https://observablehq.com/@d3/force-directed-tree?intent=fork), and to resolve [collisions](./d3-force/collide.md) as in [bubble charts](http://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2012/09/06/us/politics/convention-word-counts.html).
```
author: mbostock

body:
This should be a separate example rather than part of the existing group because it is not functionally equivalent like the other tabs are. I would add some preamble context like “you can also import individual D3 modules like so”.

Other notes:

- Use a major version number when loading d3-force
- Blank lines at the start and end of the script
- No two spaces of indentation for the script
- Use four spaces of indentation for chaining
author: mbostock

body:
It’s not a typo, but I agree it’s a little awkward. For more than a decade,

- D3 has powered groundbreaking and award-winning visualizations
- D3 has become a foundational building block
- D3 has fostered a vibrant community

Though maybe it should say “has been a foundational building block” rather than “has become a foundational building block”?
